_about: Reconciliation ledger for the register-completeness gate (verify_register_completeness.py). Every mandatory RFC-2119 keyword (MUST/MUST NOT/SHALL/SHALL NOT/REQUIRED) in the pinned prose spec must be a register row, OR listed here. This file is how we PROVE the coverage denominator is complete rather than assumed. Two kinds of entry: (1) scope_exclusions — a whole spec file whose obligations are structurally outside what a server-endpoint conformance checker can observe, or are non-normative documents (narrative/examples/templates/guides). (2) waivers — a single keyword line in an IN-SCOPE file that is a duplicate restatement, non-normative prose, or a prose MUST enforced by a schema row. A missed keyword that is neither covered by a row nor listed here FAILS the build. Reasons must be spec-grounded; a thin or bogus reason is itself rejected.

scope_exclusions.reason: Embedded-Protocol (EP) binding for checkout: a browser host-page <-> iframe integration built on window.postMessage / MessagePort. Its obligations bind the host page and the embedded UI surface, not the server's HTTP/MCP API, and are not observable by pointing a conformance checker at a server URL. A different harness (a headless browser driving the EP handshake) would be required; out of scope for this server-endpoint suite.

The Embedded Protocol (EP) itself: window.postMessage / MessagePort channel establishment and message framing between a host page and an embedded commerce UI. Purely a browser client-side transport contract; nothing here is observable at a server endpoint, so it is outside a server-conformance checker's testable surface.

Embedded-Protocol (EP) binding for cart: the browser host <-> iframe MessagePort UI protocol for embedded cart sessions. Same rationale as embedded-checkout — binds the host/embedded UI, not the server API, and is not observable from a server endpoint.

A fill-in-the-blank TEMPLATE ('# {Handler Name} Payment Handler', '{reverse-dns.name}') for authors WRITING a payment-handler specification. Its MUST/REQUIRED clauses bind the author of a handler spec (e.g. 'Specifications MUST document these field mappings'), not the merchant server under test. No server-observable obligation lives here.

An explanatory guide to the tokenization flow. The normative tokenization contract is defined in the payment-handler schemas and the tokenization OpenAPI it links to; the guide's prose keywords restate or contextualize those, and are not an independent normative source for server conformance.

A worked, illustrative example of one payment-handler configuration. Keywords describe what this specific example scenario does; they are not independent normative obligations on a UCP server. Examples are non-normative by spec convention.
A worked, illustrative example of a processor-tokenizer payment-handler configuration. Non-normative by spec convention; keywords narrate the example, not server obligations.
A worked, illustrative example of an encrypted-credential payment handler. Non-normative by spec convention; keywords narrate the example scenario, not independent server obligations.
A worked, illustrative example of a business-tokenizer payment handler. Non-normative by spec convention; keywords narrate the example scenario, not independent server obligations.

waivers.reason: Step-1 restatement 'The platform MUST verify the signature:' (pseudocode intro) is the same platform-verifies-merchant-signature obligation captured by the L110 new_row ('The platform MUST verify the business's signature before presenting'). Consistent with 2026-01-11/23 where the equivalent line waives to PAY-023.

'When the user confirms the purchase, the platform MUST facilitate the generation of ... mandates' restates the platform-provides-mandates obligation captured by the L31 new_row ('Platforms MUST provide cryptographically signed proofs (Mandates)'). Consistent with 2026-01-11/23 where the equivalent line waives to PAY-020.

'The platform MUST verify the signature:' (Step-1 verify_merchant_authorization pseudocode intro) is the same platform-verifies-merchant-signature obligation already captured by PAY-023 ('The platform MUST verify the business's signature before presenting the checkout to the user').

'When the user confirms the purchase, the platform MUST facilitate the generation of ... mandates' restates the platform-provides-mandates obligation already captured by PAY-020 ('Platforms MUST provide cryptographically signed proofs (Mandates) during the complete operation').

'The platform MUST verify the signature:' (Step-1 verify_merchant_authorization pseudocode intro) is the same platform-verifies-merchant-signature obligation already captured by PAY-023 ('The platform MUST verify the business's signature before presenting the checkout to the user').

'When the user confirms the purchase, the platform MUST facilitate the generation of ... mandates' restates the platform-provides-mandates obligation already captured by PAY-020 ('Platforms MUST provide cryptographically signed proofs (Mandates) during the complete operation').

Client-side obligation on MCP clients (the caller) to send meta['ucp-agent'] profile. Already captured transport-agnostically by DISC-006 ('Platforms MUST include their profile URI in every request using the transport-appropriate mechanism'); MCP meta is that mechanism. Not a server obligation.
Same obligation as the OpenRPC->tools/call transformation MUST at L804 (this line just introduces the worked complete_checkout example of that transformation).
Client-side meta obligation. NEG-003 already captures the identical clause ('MCP Transport: Platforms MUST include a `meta` object containing request...') from overview.md.
Same obligation as the OpenRPC->tools/call transformation MUST at L615 (introduces the worked example of that transformation).

Client-side profile-advertisement obligation ('MCP clients MUST include the UCP platform profile URI with every request'). Already captured transport-agnostically by NEG-004 ('Profile Advertisement: Platforms MUST include their profile URI in ...').
Restatement of the same client profile obligation ('_meta.ucp.profile MUST be present in every MCP tool invocation'); duplicate of NEG-004 and of L62 above.
Client-side meta obligation (MCP clients MUST include meta). Duplicate of DISC-006 client profile obligation; MCP meta is the transport-appropriate mechanism.
Client obligation ('Platforms MUST check messages before accessing order fields'). Duplicate of ORD-023 (MUST check the messages array in responses before accessing order data).
Client bullet under 'Platforms implementing the MCP binding' (MUST include meta.ucp-agent.profile on all requests). Duplicate of DISC-006 profile obligation.
Client bullet - verbatim ORD-023 quote ('MUST check the messages array in responses before accessing order data'). Duplicate of ORD-023.
Client-side meta obligation. Duplicate of DISC-006 (transport-appropriate profile mechanism).
Same identifier-pattern clause as the canonical checkout id-field row (checkout-mcp L120), applied to the `cart` object. Per shared-clause dedup, waive against the first 2026-04-08 occurrence.
Identical 'A conforming MCP transport implementation MUST:' stem as the canonical checkout conformance row (checkout-mcp L783); cart-specific bullets carry no additional RFC-2119 keywords.
Client-side meta obligation. Duplicate of DISC-006.

Platform-side mirror of the spec-origin/namespace binding: 'Platform MUST validate this binding and SHOULD reject capabilities where the spec origin does not match the namespace authority.' The server-observable constraint is the business's well-formedness obligation captured in the new L83 row. This is a client-side validation obligation, not exercised by the REST conformance surface (older versions rowed it as DISC-004).

'Vendors MUST use their own reverse-domain namespace for custom capabilities' is governance framing enforced by the spec-origin/namespace binding (new L83 row) plus the reverse-domain naming rule (new L59 row). Not a distinct server-observable obligation. Older versions rowed it as DISC-005.

'Transport definitions MUST be thin: they declare method names and reference base schemas only' is an authoring rule for the OpenAPI/OpenRPC transport-definition documents themselves, not a runtime obligation on a conforming business/platform. Restated normatively at L215. Not server-observable via the conformance HTTP surface. (Older versions rowed the analog as DISC-006.)

'Transport definitions (OpenAPI/OpenRPC) MUST reference base schemas only' governs the structure of transport-definition artifacts (spec-authoring), not the runtime behavior of a server. Duplicate of the same rule at L120. Not testable via the conformance HTTP surface.

'They MUST NOT enumerate fields or define payload shapes inline' is the negative half of the same transport-definition authoring rule (L215/L120). Concerns how schema documents are written, not runtime server behavior. (Older versions: DISC-009.)

'Extensions MUST be self-describing. Each extension schema MUST declare the types it introduces and how it modifies base types using allOf composition' is an extension-schema authoring convention, not a runtime obligation observable over HTTP. (Older versions: DISC-010.)
Second keyword occurrence on the same line 217 ('Each extension schema MUST declare...'); same extension-schema authoring convention as the first L217 occurrence. Not a runtime server obligation.

'Platforms MUST resolve schemas client-side by fetching and composing base schemas with active extension schemas' describes internal platform (client) schema-composition process. Not a business-server obligation and not observable over the conformance HTTP surface. Restated at L330/L629. (Older versions: DISC-011.)

'The $defs key MUST use the full parent capability name (reverse-domain format)' is an extension-schema authoring convention (build-time verifiable), not a runtime server obligation. Restated at L253/L255.

'Extension schemas MUST have a $defs entry for each parent declared in extends' is an extension-schema authoring rule (build-time check per the spec's own 'Verifiable' note). Not runtime server behavior.
'The $defs key MUST match the parent's full capability name exactly' duplicates the authoring convention at L225. Extension-schema authoring rule, not a runtime server obligation.

'Keys in requires.capabilities MUST be a subset of the extension's $defs keys' constrains how an extension schema author writes the requires block (self-consistency of the schema document), not runtime server behavior. Mirrored only descriptively in ucp.json (not a hard schema constraint).

'Platforms and businesses MUST verify the negotiated protocol version and capability versions satisfy the declared constraints during schema resolution' is the extension-`requires` refinement of version-compatibility verification. Its server-observable effect (incompatible extensions excluded from the active capability set) is captured by the new L1974 row (version-compatible capabilities only) and NEG-002 (capabilities_incompatible).

'Platforms MUST resolve schemas following this sequence' enumerates the client-side schema-resolution flow (Discovery/Negotiation/Fetch/Compose/Validate). Restates L220/L629; internal platform process, not server-observable.

'Platforms MUST communicate their profile URI with each request' is the profile-advertisement obligation already rowed as DISC-006 (Platforms MUST include their profile URI in every request, overview.md#L622-L623).

'HTTP Transport: Platforms MUST use Dictionary Structured Field syntax (RFC 8941) in the UCP-Agent header' is the concrete header-syntax detail already captured per-transport by CHK-046 (UCP-Agent header, RFC 8941 dictionary syntax) and at the overview level by DISC-006.

'MCP Transport: Platforms MUST include a meta object containing request metadata' is the MCP-transport mechanism of the same profile-advertisement obligation (DISC-006). MCP transport is out of the REST conformance surface (transport: rest).

'Namespace Validation: Platforms MUST validate that capability spec URI origins match namespace authorities' is the platform-side mirror of the spec-origin/namespace binding captured in the new L83 row (and L90). Client-side validation, not exercised by the REST server surface. (Older versions: NEG-007.)

'Schema Resolution: Platforms MUST fetch and compose schemas for negotiated capabilities before making requests' restates the client-side schema-resolution process (L220/L330). Internal platform behavior, not server-observable.

'Profile Resolution: businesses MUST fetch and validate the platform profile unless already cached' is the business obligation whose server-observable outcomes are already rowed: invalid_profile_url (NEG-005), profile_unreachable (NEG-003), profile_malformed (NEG-004).

'Capability Intersection: Businesses MUST compute the intersection of platform and business capabilities' is the negotiation computation whose observable output is the response's ucp.capabilities, captured by the new L950 row (and the empty-intersection error case by NEG-002). Older versions rowed it as NEG-010.

'When using Streamable HTTP transport, servers MUST return the corresponding HTTP status code alongside the JSON-RPC error' is the MCP/Streamable-HTTP variant of the standard-HTTP-status-code obligation (captured for REST at L1587 and concretely by CHK-050 and the NEG/SIG status-code rows). MCP transport is out of the REST conformance surface.
'Business-to-platform webhooks MUST be signed' is already rowed as SIG-027 (Webhook notifications MUST be signed) and ORD-026/ORD-027 (order webhook payloads MUST be signed per Message Signatures).

'If a profile cannot be fetched or fails validation, businesses MUST reject the request with an appropriate error and status code' is the general statement whose server-observable outcomes are already rowed: NEG-005 (invalid_profile_url), NEG-003 (profile_unreachable), NEG-004 (profile_malformed).

'MUST return the UCP response payload in structuredContent' is the MCP dual-output response requirement. MCP transport is out of the REST conformance surface (transport: rest); the equivalent REST obligation to return the UCP envelope is captured per-capability (e.g., CHK-034).

'All UCP communication MUST occur over HTTPS' is the umbrella transport-security statement; HTTPS is rowed per capability/transport: CART-023, CHK-045, ORD-022 (HTTPS + TLS 1.3), IDL-053 (HTTPS + TLS 1.2), and DISC-001/DISC-004 for profiles.
'businesses MUST respond with a version_unsupported error' (unsupported platform protocol version) is already rowed as NEG-001 (version_unsupported -> HTTP 422), which cites overview.md#L699,L1912.
'Businesses MUST validate the platform's protocol version on every request' is the obligation whose server-observable outcome (version_unsupported when incompatible) is rowed as NEG-001.

'The following changes MUST NOT be introduced without a new version:' (breaking-changes list) is spec-evolution governance describing how the protocol itself may change across versions, not a runtime obligation on a conforming implementation.

'Capabilities MUST follow the same backwards compatibility rules as the protocol' is versioning governance for capability authors (how capabilities evolve across versions), not a runtime server obligation.

'Businesses MUST validate capability version compatibility using the same logic as ... above' is realized through the intersection version-selection and its error outcome capabilities_incompatible (NEG-002), and the version-compatible-capabilities selection (new L1974 row).
'Composed type names MUST use the pattern: {capability-name}.{TypeName}' is a schema-authoring naming convention for composed/extension types, not a runtime server obligation observable over HTTP.

'Platforms MUST resolve schemas following this sequence' enumerates the client-side schema-resolution flow already rowed as DISC-011 (Platforms MUST resolve schemas client-side by fetching and composing base schemas with active extension schemas).

'The platform MUST now open continue_url in a WebView/Window...' is an italicized narrative step inside a worked payment-flow example (3DS/SCA soft-decline), illustrating a platform (client) action. The normative continue_url obligations are rowed separately (CHK-025/CHK-032/CHK-038).

'Business to Platform (Webhooks): Webhooks MUST be signed using a shared secret or asymmetric key' is already rowed as ORD-014 (Webhook payloads MUST be signed by the business and verified by the platform) and ORD-015.
'The following changes MUST NOT be introduced without a new version:' (breaking-changes list) is spec-evolution governance, not a runtime obligation on a conforming implementation.
'Capabilities MUST follow the same backwards compatibility rules as the protocol' is versioning governance for capability authors, not a runtime server obligation.

'Businesses MUST validate capability version compatibility using the same logic' is realized through version validation and the negotiation-failure error outcome already rowed as NEG-014 (validate platform version / determine compatibility) and NEG-013 (negotiation failure -> error response).
'Composed type names MUST use the pattern: {capability-name}.{TypeName}' is a schema-authoring naming convention, not a runtime server obligation.
'Platforms MUST resolve schemas following this sequence' is the client-side schema-resolution flow already rowed as DISC-011.

'MCP Transport: Platforms MUST use native dictionary structure in _meta.ucp' is the MCP-transport mechanism of the profile-advertisement obligation already rowed as NEG-004 (Platforms MUST include their profile URI in every request using the transport-appropriate mechanism) and NEG-001. MCP transport is out of the REST conformance surface; the 2026-01-11 register has no dedicated MCP-meta row.

'The platform MUST now open continue_url in a WebView/Window...' is an italicized narrative step in a worked payment-flow example (3DS/SCA), illustrating a platform (client) action; the normative continue_url obligations are rowed separately (CHK-025/CHK-032/CHK-038).
'Business to Platform (Webhooks): Webhooks MUST be signed using a shared secret or asymmetric key' is already rowed as ORD-014 and ORD-015.
'The following changes MUST NOT be introduced without a new version:' (breaking-changes list) is spec-evolution governance, not a runtime obligation.
'Capabilities MUST follow the same backwards compatibility rules as the protocol' is versioning governance for capability authors, not a runtime server obligation.
'Businesses MUST validate capability version compatibility using the same logic' is realized through version validation and the negotiation-failure error outcome rowed as NEG-014 and NEG-013.

Subject=platform. Intro sentence to the 'Warning Presentation' rendering-contract table: 'The presentation field on warning messages controls the rendering contract the platform MUST follow.' Narrative lead-in whose normative cells are already rowed — the overall rendering contract is ERR-012, and the individual table/prose obligations are ERR-013..ERR-022. Not a distinct server obligation (platform-side rendering, not server-observable).

Subject=platform. Table cell '| Escalate if cannot honor | — | MUST via continue_url |' is the tabular restatement of the disclosure-escalation prose at L371, already captured verbatim by ERR-018 ('Platforms that cannot honor the disclosure rendering contract MUST escalate to merchant UI via continue_url...'). Table-cell restatement of an already-rowed platform requirement.

Subject=platform. Section lead-in: 'Warnings with presentation: "disclosure" carry notices ... that MUST follow the prescribed rendering contract below.' Narrative pointer to the disclosure rendering-contract bullets that immediately follow, each already rowed (ERR-014 display content, ERR-015 proximity, ERR-016 must-not-hide, ERR-017 image_url). Not a distinct obligation.

Subject=business. 'Eligibility and policy enforcement MUST occur at checkout time using binding transaction data.' Already captured by SAE-012 (same verbatim requirement; SAE-012 cites the context schema description that mirrors this checkout.md prose). Same normative obligation, different source location.

Subject=platform. 'signal values MUST NOT be buyer-asserted claims — platforms provide signals based on direct observation or by relaying independently verifiable third-party attestations.' Already captured by SAE-001 (quotes the fuller overview.md#Signals statement of the same platform-bound MUST NOT). Platform-provenance obligation, not a business-response wire shape.

Subject=business. 'Unknown types MUST include display_text (schema-enforced).' The parenthetical explicitly flags schema enforcement and TOT-004 already captures 'unknown types MUST include display_text' with schema_enforced=true (totals.json if/then: type not in well-known enum -> require display_text).

Subject=platform (requester). 'When HTTP Message Signatures are used, requests MUST include valid Signature-Input and Signature headers (and Content-Digest when a body is present) per RFC 9421.' REST-transport restatement of the canonical signatures.md requirement rowed as SIG-013 (Signature-Input and Signature headers required on requests/responses); the Content-Digest-when-body clause is rowed as SIG-011. No distinct obligation.

Subject=business. Business-guidelines bullet '* MUST authenticate requests to order data before returning a response (see Authorization)' is the guideline restatement of the Authorization-section requirement already captured by ORD-012 (whose notes explicitly record 'Restated in Business guideline at L403'). Same authentication obligation.

Subject is 'Specifications' (payment-handler spec authors), not the merchant server: 'Specifications **MUST** explicitly document these field mappings.' Binds document authors of downstream handler specs, not a runtime server obligation; not testable via HTTP/MCP.

Subject is 'The specification' (payment-handler spec authors): 'The specification **MUST** define a mapping for common failures...' Binds handler-spec authors, not the merchant server; documentation obligation, not runtime-observable.

Under '## Specification Template': 'Sections marked **[REQUIRED]** **MUST** be present...' constrains the structure of downstream handler specification documents authored by spec authors, not a server behavior. Documentation/template checklist.

Same sentence as the REQUIRED occurrence on L762 under '## Specification Template'; the MUST binds spec-author document structure ('Sections marked [REQUIRED] MUST be present'), not the merchant server.

Bullet under '## Conformance Checklist for Spec Authors' → 'Before publishing a payment handler specification, verify: ... [ ] All [REQUIRED] sections are present'. Explicit spec-author checklist item, not a server obligation.

Under '## Specification Template' (2026-01-23): 'Sections marked **[REQUIRED]** **MUST** be present...' constrains downstream handler-spec document structure, authored by spec authors; not a server behavior.
Same L664 sentence under '## Specification Template' (2026-01-23); the MUST binds spec-author document structure, not the merchant server.
Bullet under '## Conformance Checklist for Spec Authors' (2026-01-23): '[ ] All [REQUIRED] sections are present'. Spec-author documentation checklist, not a server obligation.

Under '## Specification Template' (2026-01-11): 'Sections marked **[REQUIRED]** **MUST** be present...' constrains downstream handler-spec document structure, authored by spec authors; not a server behavior.
Same L359 sentence under '## Specification Template' (2026-01-11); the MUST binds spec-author document structure, not the merchant server.
Bullet under '## Conformance Checklist for Spec Authors' (2026-01-11): '[ ] All [REQUIRED] sections are present'. Spec-author documentation checklist, not a server obligation.

Contract-table cell 'Proximity to path | MUST' (disclosure column) summarizes the disclosure prose bullet at L359 that ERR-015 already captures: platforms MUST display the warning in proximity to the component referenced by path.

Contract-table cell 'Dismissible | MUST NOT' (disclosure column) summarizes the disclosure prose bullet at L363 that ERR-016 already captures: platforms MUST NOT hide, collapse, or auto-dismiss the disclosure warning.

Contract-table cell 'Render image_url | MUST' (disclosure column) summarizes the disclosure prose bullet at L364 that ERR-017 already captures: platforms MUST render image_url when present for disclosure warnings.

'MUST NOT alter the rendered output - the business's presented totals are authoritative' restates TOT-007's prohibition (platforms MUST NOT substitute their own computed totals for the business's values), applied here to the sum-mismatch verification case. The other MUST NOT in the block (autonomous

'The business controls what MUST be rendered (top-level entries)' restates TOT-001 (platforms MUST render all top-level totals entries in the order provided); the contrasting 'MAY optionally surface sub-lines' half is TOT-018.
The 'Platforms MUST display' clause at end of L200 is the same sentence that wraps to L201 which DSC-015 quotes verbatim: Platforms MUST display provisional discounts to the buyer.
'claims MUST be resolved' is the L227-228 sentence DSC-017 already quotes: at checkout completion, all remaining provisional claims MUST be resolved.

'If the values do not match, the platform MUST abort and discard the authorization response' is the tail of the single iss-validation bullet (L138-144). IDL-012 spans that whole bullet and its requirement explicitly includes the abort/discard-on-mismatch obligation.

'the business MUST return:' at L416 is the exact lead-in IDL-040 quotes for the identity_required error (business MUST return HTTP 401, a WWW-Authenticate: Bearer challenge, and a UCP error body with code identity_required).

'the header MUST also include error=invalid_token' is the L427-428 sentence IDL-042 already captures: when the request included an invalid/expired/unverifiable token, the WWW-Authenticate header MUST include error=invalid_token.

'the business MUST return:' at L491 is the exact lead-in IDL-045 quotes for the insufficient_scope error (business MUST return HTTP 403, a WWW-Authenticate: Bearer challenge, and a UCP error body with code insufficient_scope).
'Public clients ... cannot keep a client_secret confidential and MUST use none' is exactly IDL-050's requirement (row deliberately spans L578-585).

'Businesses MUST NOT require client_secret_basic as the only method when serving native or agent platforms' is stated verbatim in IDL-050's requirement text; IDL-050 explicitly spans L578-585 and captures both the client 'MUST use none' and this business MUST NOT.

Security-summary restatement 'Platforms MUST validate the iss parameter in the authorization response' duplicates IDL-012, the primary Mix-Up-Attack iss-validation obligation defined in the main flow (L138).

'Businesses MUST emit WWW-Authenticate: Bearer challenges ... on 401 and 403 responses' is exactly IDL-026 (businesses MUST emit a WWW-Authenticate: Bearer challenge on 401 identity_required and 403 insufficient_scope responses).
'Businesses MUST enforce exact string matching for redirect_uri' is exactly IDL-020 (businesses MUST enforce exact string matching for the redirect_uri parameter during authorization).

The order-rest conformance bullet 'MUST include UCP-Agent header with profile URL on all requests' is the identical obligation captured by ORD-015 (platforms MUST include the UCP-Agent header with profile URL on all order requests).
'MUST serve all endpoints over HTTPS with TLS 1.3+' duplicates ORD-022 (all order REST endpoints MUST be served over HTTPS with minimum TLS version 1.3).

'Implementations MUST NOT follow redirects (3xx) on profile fetches' duplicates DISC-002 (profile endpoints MUST NOT use 3xx redirects; verifiers MUST NOT follow them). Same no-redirect-on-profile-fetch obligation; the adjacent DISC-004 only covers the HTTPS-rejection item.

'Businesses MUST include the negotiated protocol version in every response' is captured by OVR-004 (businesses MUST include the ucp field in every response, containing the processing version and active capabilities); ucp.version IS the negotiated protocol version.

'Pre-release implementations ... MUST NOT be surfaced through public discovery' duplicates OVR-010 (protocol version MUST be a dated YYYY-MM-DD release; businesses MUST NOT advertise a non-date/draft version string in their profile version field or supported_versions).
'Implementations MUST use sha-256' is explicitly part of SIG-010's requirement: Content-Digest follows RFC 9530 and hashes the raw body bytes; implementations MUST use sha-256.

'Transport definitions (OpenAPI/OpenRPC) MUST reference base schemas only' is the affirmative half of the same single rule DISC-009 captures from its negative half (L198): transport definitions MUST NOT enumerate fields or define payload shapes inline.
'The business MUST NOT accept a complete_checkout request that lacks ap2.checkout_mandate' (L107-108) is exactly the obligation PAY-022 captures for negotiated ap2_mandate.

'MUST sign all webhook payloads using a key from their signing_keys array' (L331-332) is exactly ORD-015 (businesses MUST sign all webhook payloads with a signing_keys key published in /.well-known/ucp, signature carried in Request-Signature as a detached JWT).
